channel.title: Religious Studies Posts on My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content in Religious Studies Posts on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Book Review: The Science of Being Great
item.description: I finished reading the book The Science of Being Great by the American author Wallace Wattles (1860 - 1911). Wallace Wattles was a prolific American author in the self-help genre who put out his best works in the early 20th century. This might be an early book in the self-help genre, which is a relatively recent phenomena because in past generations, people would often read history or religious texts if they wanted to learn something new that would help them. This is a good book that definitely should be categorized as Religious Studies. Why? Because this book was really about how to be great not as the world defines it, but what true greatness is from a spiritual lens as defined by the Bible. As a practicing Christian, this is exactly the type of book I enjoy reading and learning from. I believe that we’ve perhaps lost sight of what it really means to be great in modern day 2026 and in our society overall. Maybe, just maybe, we are all chasing the wrong things and we are not as great as we think we are unless we align ourselves spiritually with the Creator. Wattles walks the reader over how to be great and breaks it down into an exact science, and I think the book is a fantastic one overall. I’m going to summarize the key points and save some quotes I want to remember for future reference.
